deck.name: Phrasal Verbs Masterclass
deck.description: Master the most common and useful phrasal verbs for daily English communication.

cards.definition: to manage to live or survive with very little money or knowledge
cards.context: I don't speak much Japanese, but I know enough to get by.

cards.definition: to be excited about something that is going to happen
cards.context: I'm really looking forward to our trip next month.

cards.definition: to mention a topic in a conversation
cards.context: I didn't want to bring up the budget during the meeting.

cards.definition: to cancel something that was planned
cards.context: They had to call off the football match because of the rain.

cards.definition: to tolerate or accept something unpleasant without complaining
cards.context: I don't know how she puts up with his constant complaining.

cards.definition: to stop doing something or stop trying
cards.context: Don't give up! You're so close to reaching your goal.

cards.definition: to continue doing something
cards.context: Please carry on with your work; don't let me interrupt you.

cards.definition: to discover information or a fact
cards.context: How did you find out about the surprise party?

cards.definition: to finish the supply of something
cards.context: We've run out of milk; I'll have to go to the shop.

cards.definition: to happen in a particular way or have a particular result
cards.context: The weather was terrible in the morning, but it turned out to be a sunny day.

cards.definition: to stop working (for a machine or vehicle)
cards.context: My car broke down on the motorway this morning.

cards.definition: to talk to someone you haven't seen for a while to hear their latest news
cards.context: Let's grab a coffee next week and catch up!

cards.definition: to look at or investigate something interesting
cards.context: You should check out that new restaurant; the food is amazing.

cards.definition: to find something by chance
cards.context: I came across some old photos while I was cleaning the attic.

cards.definition: to rely on or trust someone
cards.context: You can always count on me if you need any help.

cards.definition: to prepare a formal document (e.g., a contract or plan)
cards.context: Our lawyer will draw up the contract by tomorrow morning.

cards.definition: to fail to happen (for a plan or deal)
cards.context: The house sale fell through at the last minute.

cards.definition: to complete a form by writing information in the spaces
cards.context: Please fill in your details on this form.

cards.definition: to have a good relationship with someone
cards.context: I get along very well with my new colleagues.

cards.definition: to finally agree to something you were refusing; to surrender
cards.context: After hours of begging, her parents finally gave in and bought her the toy.

cards.definition: to review or examine something carefully
cards.context: Let's go over the main points of the presentation one more time.

cards.definition: to move or progress at the same rate as someone or something
cards.context: It's hard to keep up with all the new technology these days.

cards.definition: to investigate or research something
cards.context: The police are looking into the cause of the accident.

cards.definition: to invent a story or excuse
cards.context: He made up a crazy story about why he was late for work.

cards.definition: to lose consciousness
cards.context: It was so hot in the room that I felt like I was going to pass out.

cards.definition: to collect someone or something from a place
cards.context: Can you pick me up from the station at 6 PM?

cards.definition: to organize or arrange something
cards.context: We need to set up a meeting to discuss the new project.

cards.definition: to leave the ground (for an aircraft) or to become successful quickly
cards.context: The plane took off on time. / Her business really took off last year.

cards.definition: to exercise or to solve a problem
cards.context: I try to work out at least three times a week. / We'll work out a solution together.

cards.definition: to record information on paper
cards.context: Could you write down your phone number for me?

cards.definition: to move backwards or to make a copy of computer data
cards.context: You should always back up your important files.

cards.definition: to explode or to start to shout at someone because you are angry
cards.context: The bomb was designed to blow up on impact. / He blew up at me when I told him the news.

cards.definition: to make someone feel unhappy or to cause a government or politician to lose power
cards.context: This sad music is really bringing me down.

cards.definition: to become extremely tired or sick by working too hard
cards.context: If you don't take a break soon, you're going to burn out.

cards.definition: to become or make someone become less excited or angry
cards.context: Take a deep breath and try to calm down.

cards.definition: to start to feel happier or to make someone feel happier
cards.context: I bought you some flowers to cheer you up.

cards.definition: to stop operating (for a business or organization)
cards.context: The old cinema closed down years ago.

cards.definition: to reduce the amount or quantity of something
cards.context: I'm trying to cut back on caffeine lately.

cards.definition: to stop participating in something, especially a school or competition
cards.context: He dropped out of university after only one semester.

cards.definition: to finally be in a particular place or situation
cards.context: We took a wrong turn and ended up in a completely different town.

cards.definition: to break into pieces or to experience a serious emotional or structural failure
cards.context: My old shoes are starting to fall apart.

cards.definition: to understand or find a solution to something
cards.context: I can't figure out how to use this new software.

cards.definition: to recover from an illness, loss, or difficult experience
cards.context: It took her months to get over the breakup.

cards.definition: to give something to a person in authority
cards.context: Please hand in your assignments by the end of the week.

cards.definition: to spend time relaxing or socializing in a particular place
cards.context: We used to hang out at the park every afternoon.
